actionable_feedback: Open reactive_distillation_screen.xlsx in a spreadsheet program and verify the cost sums and the 'Within budget' boolean for every decision-row; correct any incorrect boolean or sum cells (specifically the O2+O3 row).
Make the 'Within budget' flag formulaic (e.g., =SUM(option_cost_cells) <= remediation_budget_usd) so flags cannot be inconsistent with numeric sums.
Add row- and cell-level citations in process_option_memo.md mapping each derived field, each option cost, and each decision-table flag back to the exact workbook cell (sheet name and cell address).

Add a short appendix to the memo showing the +/-5% sensitivity recalculation: include the raw adjusted metric values, the re-normalized metric results, the recalculated scores, and any changed classification for each record so reviewers can validate the sensitivity check quickly.

Ensure the workbook includes visible validation cells: normalization_denominator_nonzero, explicit normalization formulas (with references to the min/max source cells), and an 'audit' sheet listing key formulas in plain text for manual reviewers.

If you used manual OOXML generation because openpyxl was unavailable, confirm workbook compatibility by opening and saving it in Excel or LibreOffice to ensure formulas evaluate as intended; if possible regenerate the XLSX using a standard library (openpyxl/xlsxwriter) before resubmission.

Update grader/verifier metadata to remove internal contradictions (e.g., duplicate ri_1 entries and conflicting 'passed' flags) and include an explicit note in the memo that the workbook has been validated manually (with software/version used).
